Lifelong citizen of abudhabi and super fan of etihad here ahem Platinum s Its true that UAE is under attack but I would hardly call it a total war zone At the point the occasional interceptor boom every few hours is nothing but an icebreaker for a conversation Every now and then you hear an interceptor but nothing really serious Life is still going on pretty normally Beach clubs are still banging malls packed I would say the war here in Abu Dhabi is much like the war in Moscow sure theres the RussoUkranian war but life is pretty much normal in Moscow Out of over 2000 missiles and drones there barely has been any damage whatsoever I hope this alleviates your VERY UNDERSTANDABLE concerns its just that it is a tad exaggerated Back to Etihad I would say etihad are going above and beyond with refunds Sure the refund page is whimsical and the chat is a test of patience but they are still actively refunding and rescheduling My flight was cancelled by local aviation authorities at the last minute due to risks Got a full refund AND got my Buy One Get One free voucher reinstated with a validity of one year from date of issuance I would argue the only reason flights are still departing out of UAE is because of our 90 expat population who are trying to go back home as things settle Flights out of the UAE are overbooked Inbound however just saw a post of a girl who was one of just 12 on an Emirates A380 So relax Cancel or postpone your flight for now the flights are already limited and priority is for evacuation But never bet against the gulf aviation giant
